tavily.answer: The Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is facing a complex crisis marked by ongoing conflict and a severe Ebola outbreak. The latest Ebola outbreak has seen nearly 300 people with the virus unaccounted for, according to Africa’s top public health official. The humanitarian crisis is exacerbated by the conflict, with over a million people living in inaccessible camps. In Ituri province, violence has erupted as protesters demanded access to the bodies of Ebola victims, leading to the burning of an Ebola hospital. The World Health Organization (WHO) has warned of a "catastrophic collision" between the disease and conflict, with the outbreak in Ituri outpacing the response. Additionally, armed rebel groups have killed at least 69 people in Ituri province, further destabilizing the region. The DRC's response to the Ebola outbreak is being hampered by ongoing conflicts and mass displacement, which have weakened the health system and made access to affected areas difficult.

items.title: DRC Ebola hospital set on fire as protesters demand access to bodies of dead relatives - africanews.com
items.snippet: **Violence has erupted in eastern DR Congo's Ituri province after tensions linked to the handling of victims of the latest Ebola outbreak.**. Protesters in the town of Rwampara wanted to recover the bodies of relatives who died of Ebola in order to bury them.
